topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification flashed on my screen, a stark, unwelcome intrusion into the quiet rhythm of my evening. It was from Mark. My stomach tightened immediately, a cold knot forming beneath my ribs. I stared at the profile picture—the same sneering grin I remembered from the playground years—and my fingers hovered over the 'Accept' button, then retreated. A wave of heat crept up my neck, making my skin prickle. Why now? After all this time, after I’d built a life where that memory felt distant, this digital tether felt like a deliberate yank back into the past. I quickly minimized the message, shoving my phone face down on the desk, wishing the floor would swallow me whole.

[story 2]

When the alert pinged, I nearly dropped my coffee mug. It was Mark. The name alone sent a tremor through my hands, making the ceramic feel suddenly slick. I picked up the device slowly, my gaze darting between the profile picture and the request itself. My breath hitched, shallow and quick. A flush spread across my cheeks, hot and undeniable. I felt a desperate urge to delete the application entirely, to erase the possibility of interaction. I fiddled with the edge of the table, my knuckles turning white as I fought the urge to slam the phone down in frustration. Every nerve ending seemed to be screaming a warning, a silent plea to ignore this unwelcome connection.

[story 3]

I saw the request pop up, and my entire posture shifted. My shoulders hunched inward, a protective shield snapping into place around my chest. Looking at Mark’s name, a dull, heavy weight settled in my chest, making it difficult to draw a full breath. My throat felt suddenly dry. I spent a long minute just staring at the screen, the silence in the room amplifying the frantic thumping in my ears. I rehearsed a dozen different ways to ignore it, but the sheer familiarity of the request made it impossible. I finally tapped 'View Profile,' my eyes scanning the familiar, mocking details, a visible tremor running through my hands as I processed the unwelcome intrusion.
